hero.title: One Health Data Space Demonstrator Center
hero.subtitle: More than data

metadata.title: Demostrador
metadata.description: Explicación de que es el demostrador

header.title: What is the Demonstrator?
header.lead: It is a platform that allows sharing and accessing health data in a secure, ethical, governed and interoperable manner, respecting data sovereignty

objective.title: Demonstrator Objective
objective.text1: The demonstrator aims to illustrate how multiple organizations in the biotechnology and health sectors can exchange data safely and following controlled protocols.
objective.text2: The system ensures regulatory compliance, data sovereignty, and interoperability between different actors, from hospitals and research centers to private companies.

motivation.title: Motivation
motivation.text: Challenges in human, animal, and environmental health are interconnected and require a joint data-driven vision to address them. This demonstrator provides a platform to integrate and analyze multisectoral information, facilitating informed decisions and evidence-based public policies.

funding.title: Funding Information
funding.call: Call: Ministry for Digital Transformation and Public Administration: Demonstrators and Use Cases of Data Spaces.
funding.grant: Grant call: "Sectoral Data Spaces 2023 for the creation of demonstrators and use cases of data sharing spaces."
funding.program: The "Sectoral Data Spaces" program is part of Investment 1 of Component 12 of the Recovery, Transformation, and Resilience Plan, funded through the Recovery and Resilience Mechanism (MRR).
funding.details:
File number: TSI-100120-2024-12
Period: 13-12-2024 – 30-06-2026
Funded by: Ministry for Digital Transformation and Public Administration
Total amount: €3,077,058

metadata.title: MVD
metadata.description: Explanation of what the MVD is

header.title: Data Space (MVD version)
header.lead: The MVD brings together the essential capabilities to launch a federated data space in a secure and scalable way.

whatIs.title: What is the MVD?
whatIs.text1: The MVD is the first operational version of the Data Space that brings together the minimum set of capabilities needed to launch the federation: identity and access, data discovery, basic governance, traceability, and connectivity.
whatIs.text2: It serves to learn quickly, measure impact, and adjust the design before scaling up.

features.title: What does this phase include?
features.iam: Federated identity and access management (IAM): authentication and authorization based on policies and roles.
features.governance: Agreements and governance: Data Sharing Agreement templates and usage policies.
features.connectors: EDC connectors for integration and sandbox environment.
features.observability: Observability: access logging, auditing, and consent traceability.
features.support: Support for pilot use cases, on demand.

videos.title: Explore the MVD in action
videos.workshop23marchCaption1: PART 1: Training to carry out the necessary configuration for connector deployment.
videos.workshop23marchCaption2: PART 2: Training to carry out the necessary configuration for connector deployment.
videos.workshop1Caption: Practical workshop on how a data space operates in the FarmaciaVAX use case with the Data Space MVD.
videos.workshop2Caption: Data space workshop on EDC Connectors.
videos.workshop3Caption: PharmacyVAX data space workshop with the SQS D-Spacer platform
videos.tutorial1Caption: Step-by-step tutorial on uploading and visualizing assets using the Data Space MVD.

metadata.title: Join
metadata.description: How to join the data space: who can participate, steps to join, commitments and benefits

header.title: Participate in the data space
header.lead: Share, consume or develop data-based services in a trusted environment that guarantees sovereignty, privacy and interoperability

guideButton.label: Check our guide for participants
guideButton.href: /adhesion/Guía del participante - adhesión EdD DATAlife CESGA.pdf

who.title: Who can participate?
items: Hospitals, research centers and universities
Public administrations and entities
Companies, startups and technology centers
National and European collaborations

roles.title: Types of Participation
items: Data providers who contribute data to the ecosystem.
Service providers who offer tools or capabilities.
Data consumers who use data according to the provider’s conditions.
Service consumers who use services according to the provider’s conditions.

benefits.title: Benefits
items: Integration with European actors and data spaces
Provision of common services: catalogs, anonymization, analysis
Opportunity to establish new collaborations and alliances

commitments.title: Commitments
items: Comply with the governance framework and the rules of access and use
Guarantee data quality and traceability
Respect privacy, security and ethics regulations
Report incidents or disputes through the enabled channels

howToJoin.title: Start the membership process
howToJoin.subtitle: I am interested in joining
howToJoin.description: If you’re already informed and would like to express your interest in joining the Data Space, please fill out the following form. If you have any questions, contact us at onehealth@cesga.es.
howToJoin.formButton: Fill out the expression of interest form if you’re interested in joining

items.title: Get informed
items.description: Download the Participant Guide to learn in detail how the Data Space works, the technical and legal requirements, and all the benefits of joining. We are here to guide and support you through every step of the process.

items.title: Submit your application
items.description: Fill out the <aForm>expression of interest form</aForm>, and our team will review your request and contact you for the next steps.

items.title: Technical integration
items.description: Connect your systems and prepare your data to comply with the interoperability, quality, and security policies of the Data Space. We will be by your side to assist you throughout the process.

items.title: Participate and grow within the ecosystem
items.description: Once you are part of it, you will be able to exchange data, join use cases, and collaborate with other participants. We will continue to support you at every stage.

reviewCycle.title: Review cycle
reviewCycle.description: Applications are evaluated every two weeks during our review meeting. Only the ones submitted up to the day before (inclusive) will be considered.
reviewCycle.footer: *Days in green correspond to review meetings.

metadata.title: Team
metadata.description: Driving innovation from CESGA. Our mission is to accelerate the digital transformation of the biotechnology, agrifood, forestry-sea-industrial and health sectors.

header.title: Driving innovation from CESGA
header.missionText1: At CESGA, our mission is to accelerate the digital transformation of the biotechnology, agrifood, forestry-sea-industrial and health sectors. Through the multisectoral demonstrator, we provide solutions based on artificial intelligence, big data and high-performance computing.
header.missionText2: This project seeks to exploit, through real cases, emerging technologies that can improve the competitiveness of Galician companies, betting on a connected, sustainable and leading Galicia in innovation.

celiaspace.objective: Data from conversational assistants with seniors

metadata.title: Data Space
metadata.description: Explanation of what a data space is

header.title: What is a data space?
header.lead: A data space is a structured framework that allows the exchange, access, management and use of data between multiple entities in a secure, interoperable and compliant manner

objective.title: Objective
objective.text: The objective is to facilitate data-based collaboration, respecting principles such as data sovereignty, privacy and ethics. Data spaces combine technologies such as exchange infrastructures, common standards and access control, together with legal and organizational agreements, to allow for shared and responsible use.

motivation.title: Motivation
motivation.text: The great social, economic and environmental challenges are often interconnected and require a joint vision, based on data, to tackle them. Data spaces provide a framework to integrate and analyze information from different sectors, which facilitates collaboration between actors, informed decision-making and the development of evidence-based policies and services.

security.title: Security
security.description: Comprehensive data protection through encryption and authentication.

interoperability.title: Interoperability
interoperability.description: Integration between platforms and systems through open standards.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: Data access based on consent and transparent policies.

governance.title: Governance
governance.description: Clear rules on how information is accessed, used and shared.

collaboration.title: Collaboration
collaboration.description: Cooperation between public, private and citizen sectors.

sovereignty.title: Data sovereignty
sovereignty.description: Each organization maintains control over its own data.

metadata.title: Events
metadata.description: Stay updated with upcoming events and presentations

header.title: Events
header.description: Stay updated with upcoming events and presentations

egi2025.excerpt: Participation in the European distributed computing conference EGI 2025
description: Participation in the European distributed computing conference EGI 2025, held in Santander.
Within this framework, the Multisectoral One Health Demonstrator Center promoted by CESGA was presented, highlighting its role as a data space focused on research and innovation.
The event brought together scientific communities, service providers, and European initiatives to discuss digital infrastructures, data analysis, and new challenges in research.

datalife.header: Presentation Day of the Multisectoral Demonstrator DATAlife
datalife.excerpt: Launch event for the Multisectoral One Health Demonstrator Center (DATAlife)
description: Launch event of the Multisectoral One Health Demonstrator Center (DATAlife) in Santiago de Compostela.
The day focused on showcasing how this data space promotes research and innovation within the One Health framework, with presentations, use case showcases, and collaboration opportunities.
It also included a hands-on workshop on data space deployment, with participation from the research community, companies, and public administrations.

baidata.excerpt: Participation in the national forum on data economy and European data spaces
description: Participation in the 4th BAIDATA Forum, held in Bilbao and focused on the data economy and European data spaces.
The event gathered professionals, companies, and public administrations to discuss interoperability, governance, and use cases in sectors such as health, mobility, and industry.
It offered broad networking opportunities with other national projects, including bilateral meetings and a second coordination workshop with the Ministry.

gaiaxsalud.header: Workshop of the Gaia-X Spain Health Working Group
gaiaxsalud.excerpt: Session on the challenges and opportunities of the European Health Data Space regulation
description: Participation in the workshop of the health working group organized by Gaia-X Spain in collaboration with the Polytechnic University of Madrid.
The session, covering legal, technical, and healthcare perspectives, focused on the challenges and opportunities arising from the new European Health Data Space regulation.
The goal was to identify solutions for its implementation.

normaune.header: Presentation of the UNE-ISO/IEC 42001 Standard on AI Management
normaune.excerpt: Conference on the first international standard for artificial intelligence management systems
description: Attendance at the event organized by the Spanish Association for Standardization (UNE) and sponsored by the State Secretariat for Digitalization and Artificial Intelligence (SEDIA).
The meeting served to present the UNE-ISO/IEC 42001 standard, the first international framework establishing requirements for implementing an AI management system within organizations.
The session addressed the regulatory context and the benefits of applying this framework to ensure the ethical, safe, and transparent use of AI.

tallercred.excerpt: Meeting for beneficiaries of the Demonstrator Centers and Use Cases call
description: Attendance at an in-person workshop exclusively for beneficiaries of the Demonstrator Centers and Use Cases call, held at the CRED headquarters in Madrid.
The session clarified administrative questions regarding the projects.
It officially presented the Data Spaces Kit.

tallersqs.header: FarmaciaVAX Data Space Workshop with SQS’s D-Spacer Platform
tallersqs.excerpt: Hands-on workshop on the operation of a data space using the FarmaciaVAX use case
description: Workshop focused on demonstrating how a data space operates. It centers on the FarmaciaVAX use case, part of the Multisectoral Demonstrator Center DATAlife, and uses the D-Spacer technology developed by SQS.

The session includes an introduction to the platform and its integration into the data space context, participant onboarding and activation, policy and contract reviews to understand various constraints, and practical examples of data sharing between pharmacies and health services.
It also covers the role of the Governance Authority in validating requests and ends with a Q&A session.

kitdatoscred.header: Launch of the CRED Data Kit
kitdatoscred.excerpt: Event to present the Data Kit by CRED
description: Attendance at the session on the Data Spaces Kit to facilitate the onboarding of public and private organizations into data spaces with financial support. The session outlined the covered activities (opportunity identification, data preparation, technical connection, and legal/governance management) and the eligible expenses (personnel, contracted services, and products).
The application process—awarded on a first-come, first-served basis—was explained.
kitdatoscred.extra: If you want to refresh the event information or didn’t have the chance to watch it, don’t miss it!

kitdatoscred2.excerpt: Hands-on workshop to prepare and submit an application for the Data Spaces Kit
description: If your company is a potential beneficiary of the Data Spaces Kit, you’ll be interested to know that the upcoming call for grants of up to €30,000 will soon be launched for participants and promoters of data spaces.

This online session will have a very practical approach, where they will explain everything you need to know about the Data Spaces Kit and provide key insights to properly prepare and submit your grant application.
📅 Date: October 30, 2025
🕔 Time: 10:00 a.m.
📍 Format: Online

tallergradiant.header: Workshop on Data Spaces FarmaciaVAX with the XDataShare Platform
tallergradiant.excerpt: Hands-on workshop to understand, step by step, how a data space works by applying it to the synthetic use case FarmaciaVAX with the XDataShare platform.
description: Practical workshop to understand the real operation of a data space. We will focus on the synthetic use case FarmaciaVAX, framed within the Multisector Demonstrator Center DATAlife, using XDataShare technology.

The session includes an introduction to the platform and its integration into the data space ecosystem, the process of joining and activating participants, a review of policies and contracts to understand constraints, and practical examples of data sharing between pharmacies and health services.
We will address the role of the Governance Authority in validating requests and will conclude with a final Q&A session.
📅 Date: October 31, 2025
🕔 Time: 11:00 a.m.
📍 Format: Online

tallergradiantconectores.header: Data Space Workshop on EDC Connectors
tallergradiantconectores.excerpt: Hands-on workshop to explore connectors based on EDC (Eclipse Dataspace Components) technology using the XDataShare platform.
description: From the DATAlife Multisector Demonstration Center, we present a new Data Space session. This time, we will address connectors based on EDC (Eclipse Dataspace Components) technology, one of the key elements to achieve sovereign and interoperable data sharing.
The session includes a description of an EDC connector, explaining its basic structure and main code components.
📅 Date: November 13, 2025
🕔 Time: 10:00 AM
📍 Format: online

webinarBaidataCESGA.header: Creating a One Health Data Space: CESGA’s Experience
webinarBaidataCESGA.excerpt: Presentation of the DATAlife Multisector Demonstrator Center for secure and sovereign data sharing in One Health.
description: CESGA presents the DATAlife Multisector Demonstrator Center in the latest BAIDATA 'Data in Action' session, enabling different One Health organizations to exchange data securely and in a controlled manner.
📅 Date: November 26th, 2025
🕔 Time: 10:00 AM
📍 Format: Online
Session open to the public.

kitDatosSaludCRED.header: Practical Workshop: Data Space Kit for the Health Sector
kitDatosSaludCRED.excerpt: Free online workshop for the health sector on the Data Space Kit.
description: This initiative from the General Data Directorate will facilitate the effective incorporation of public and private sector entities into data spaces. The key steps to apply for this support will be explained, with the goal of promoting participation in health data spaces.
📅 Date: December 16, 2025
🕔 Time: 1:00 PM
📍 Format: online
Open session for the general public.

conectorGestionActivos.header: Practical Workshop on the One Health Data Space: Connector and Asset Management
conectorGestionActivos.excerpt: Free online workshop aimed at the healthcare sector about the Data Spaces Kit.
description: Do you want to be part of the One Health Data Space but are not sure how to get started?

From the ONE HEALTH Multisector Data Space Demonstrator Center, we are organizing a training session aimed at resolving doubts and facilitating the integration into the One Health Data Space for institutions, companies, and interested entities, especially those applying for the CRED Data Spaces Kit program: Reference Center for Data Spaces.
In this workshop, we will practically cover the key elements to participate in the CESGA One Health Data Space, including:
- connector deployment
- asset upload (data assets)
- evidence declaration
All of this will be supported by a real example that will help you understand step by step how this process is carried out.
This session is designed to provide a clear and useful foundation to enable active participation in the One Health Data Space.
🗓️Date: March 23
📍 Format: Online

formacionDesarrolloExtensiones.header: Advanced training on extension development for use cases
formacionDesarrolloExtensiones.excerpt: Online training to understand how the Eclipse Dataspace Connector (EDC) works, how it can be extended through extensions, and how to develop custom modules to adapt it to new business and integration needs.
description: This training will help you understand how the Eclipse Dataspace Connector (EDC) works, how it can be extended through extensions, and how to develop custom modules to adapt it to new requirements.
What is the EDC connector?
It is an open-source technology that enables secure and controlled data exchange between organizations, connecting different systems while maintaining data sovereignty.
What is it used for?
It automates data exchange by applying usage, access, and sharing policies. It is key in data spaces as it ensures security, traceability, interoperability, and compliance with conditions.
Who is it for?
Technical profiles such as developers, architects, integrators, and data platform managers, as well as anyone involved in data exchange and interoperability projects.
Where is it applied?

In projects involving heterogeneous data integration, development of custom connectors, and definition of data-sharing policies, especially in data federation environments, collaborative ecosystems, and Gaia-X.
📅 Date: April 29, 2026
📍 Format: Online
📺 The session will later be available on the CESGA YouTube channel

evolucionEspacioDatos.header: Evolution of OneHealth DataSpace: integration with high-performance file systems
evolucionEspacioDatos.excerpt: Online training to learn about one of the latest developments in the OneHealth DataSpace ecosystem: integration with high-performance storage systems at CESGA such as Lustre and NetApp.
description: Session objective
To explore one of the latest developments in the OneHealth DataSpace ecosystem: integration with high-performance storage systems at CESGA such as Lustre and NetApp.
The session will demonstrate how to use the selected solution to access folders and files hosted on HPC infrastructures in a simple and user-friendly graphical way.
Within the OneHealth DataSpace environment, this integration will enable the creation of assets using data stored directly in high-performance storage systems.
What are HPC storage systems?
CESGA provides high-performance storage systems such as:
▸ User home directories
▸ Lustre
Infrastructure associated with the FinisTerrae III supercomputer
More information about CESGA HPC storage systems:
👉 https://www.cesga.es/infraestructuras/almacenamiento/
What will be covered in the session?
▸ Integration with HPC file systems
▸ Solution architecture within the OneHealth DataSpace ecosystem
▸ Platform access
▸ Main functionalities
▸ Practical demo
Who is it for?
Aimed at any user interacting with tools in the OneHealth DataSpace ecosystem who wants to learn about this solution.
Especially useful for use cases working with data hosted in HPC environments.
📅 Date: May 8, 2026
📍 Location: CESGA Seminar
👤 Delivered by: Cecilia Grela and David Martínez
📺 The session video will be available soon on CESGA's YouTube channel (@CesgaTV)

usoPractico.header: Practical Use of AnonymiX, Clever and the Participant Portal
usoPractico.excerpt: Training session showcasing how to use tools from the OneHealth DataSpace ecosystem to securely share, manage and protect data in an interoperable way aligned with data sovereignty principles and regulatory compliance
description: Session objective
Show how to use tools from the OneHealth DataSpace ecosystem to securely share, manage and protect data in an interoperable way aligned with data sovereignty principles and regulatory compliance.
The session will provide an understanding of how to apply these solutions in real collaboration scenarios within the data space.
What are AnonymiX, Clever and the Participant Portal?
▸ AnonymiX enables the anonymization and protection of sensitive data while preserving its analytical value.
▸ Clever and the Participant Portal facilitate participant management, access to resources and interaction with the OneHealth DataSpace ecosystem.
What will be covered during the session?
▸ Using AnonymiX for data anonymization and protection
▸ Main functionalities of Clever
▸ Operation of the Participant Portal
▸ Best practices for sharing and consuming data
▸ Use cases applied to the One Health environment
▸ Integration of tools within the federated architecture of the CESGA data space
Who is it aimed at?
Target audience:
▸ Organizations already participating in or interested in joining OneHealth DataSpace
▸ Professionals from the healthcare, technology, research and biotechnology sectors
▸ Technical staff and data managers
▸ Companies, startups and technology centers
▸ Students and professionals interested in data spaces, interoperability and privacy applied to AI
📅 Date: May 15, 2026
🕚 Time: 11:00 AM - 1:00 PM
📍 Location: CESGA Seminar Room
📺 The session video will later be available on the CESGA YouTube channel (@CesgaTV)

evolucionEspacioDatosHPCBigDataCloud.header: Evolution of the OneHealth DataSpace: integration with HPC, Big Data and Cloud resources
evolucionEspacioDatosHPCBigDataCloud.excerpt: Session focused on understanding how OneHealth DataSpace evolves into an integrated platform capable of sharing, storing, processing and analyzing data through HPC, Big Data, Cloud and AI technologies within the CESGA ecosystem
description: Session objective:

Understand how OneHealth DataSpace evolves as an integrated platform for the complete One Health data lifecycle: sharing, storing, processing, analyzing and delivering results as services or applied solutions.
Unlike a conventional data space, the ecosystem integrates advanced CESGA capabilities in HPC, Big Data, Cloud, AI, GPU and quantum computing to expand data processing and exploitation possibilities.
The session will explain how the data space integrates with:
▸ High Performance Computing (HPC)
▸ Large-scale data processing through Big Data technologies
▸ Cloud environments for deploying services and applications
What will be covered during the session?
▸ Integration with HPC resources
▸ Data processing and analysis using Big Data technologies
▸ Cloud evolution of the OneHealth DataSpace ecosystem
▸ Integrated technological architecture
▸ Advanced processing and analysis capabilities
▸ Use cases and data exploitation possibilities
What are these capabilities for?
▸ Transforming data into applied solutions
▸ Expanding processing and analysis capabilities
▸ Enabling new services within the One Health ecosystem
▸ Integrating advanced computing, data and AI within the same technological infrastructure
Who is this session intended for?
Target audience:
▸ Organizations already participating or interested in joining OneHealth DataSpace
▸ Active or developing use cases
▸ Professionals interested in HPC, Big Data, Cloud, AI and data spaces
▸ Technical staff, researchers and students
📅 Date: May 22, 2026
📍 Location: https://seminar.cesga.es/
🎓 Instructors: Cecilia Grela, David Martinez and Martín Curty
📺 The session video will later be available on the CESGA YouTube channel (@CesgaTV)

gobernanzaDeDatos.excerpt: Session to understand how data is governed within OneHealth DataSpace and why governance is key to ensuring trust, interoperability, data sovereignty, and regulatory compliance within the CESGA ecosystem
description: Session objective:
Understand how data is governed within OneHealth DataSpace and why governance is a fundamental element to ensure trust, traceability, interoperability, and regulatory compliance within the ecosystem.
OneHealth DataSpace integrates advanced CESGA capabilities in HPC, Big Data, Cloud, AI, GPU, and quantum computing to share, store, process, and analyze data under the One Health approach.
The session will help understand the mechanisms that enable different organizations to share and use data securely, in a sovereign way, and in compliance with European regulations.
What is Data Governance?
▸ Data governance defines how data is managed, protected, shared, and used throughout its lifecycle.
▸ In OneHealth DataSpace, it establishes the rules, responsibilities, and guarantees required for different entities to collaborate while maintaining control over their data.
▸ It enables the operation of the data space by ensuring security, data sovereignty, transparency, and regulatory compliance.
What will be covered in the session?
▸ OneHealth DataSpace governance model: governance structure, roles, responsibilities, and the FRAND principle.
▸ Applicable regulatory framework: GDPR, Data Governance Act (DGA), EHDS, AI Act, ENS, and NIS2.
▸ Contractual instruments: Governance Framework, Accession Agreement, and Specific Terms of Use.
▸ Lifecycle of a data exchange: publication, negotiation, access, and traceability.
▸ Data protection and non-custodial architecture.
▸ Practical cases and Q&A session.
What is data governance for?
▸ Ensuring secure and traceable data exchange.
▸ Defining who can access which data and under what conditions.
▸ Protecting both data providers and data consumers.
▸ Enabling collaboration between entities with legal and technical certainty.
▸ Allowing complex data spaces to operate under European regulations.
Who is it for?
Target audience:
▸ Entities already part of or interested in joining OneHealth DataSpace.
▸ Active or developing use cases.
▸ Professionals related to governance, interoperability, and data protection.
▸ Technical staff, legal experts, and data managers.
▸ Researchers, companies, and students interested in data spaces.
📅 Date: 29 May 2026
📍 Format: Online
🎓 Instructor: Alejo Santolino Simón, Data Legal Framework and Business Analysis Coordinator
⚠️ Registration is mandatory to join the session and receive the participation certificate.
📺 The session video will be available later on the CESGA YouTube channel (@CesgaTV)

catalog.title: Data Catalog
catalog.subtitle: Explore the list of datasets and their preliminary information

aidatamed_synthetic_patients.name: Synthetic patient data
aidatamed_synthetic_patients.description: Dataset of synthetic data that reproduces patients’ clinical information generated by the AI models of the AIDataMed project. Includes demographics, diagnoses, and clinical events following the OMOP CDM model.

person.name: Patient demographics
person.description: Synthetic patient demographics, including unique identifiers, gender, date of birth, race, and ethnicity.

variables.name: person_id
variables.data_description: Unique identifier of the synthetic patient.

variables.name: gender_concept_id
variables.data_description: Patient gender concept according to the OMOP vocabulary. Example: 8532 (Female), 8507 (Male).

variables.name: year_of_birth
variables.data_description: Year of birth of the synthetic patient.

variables.name: month_of_birth
variables.data_description: Month of birth of the synthetic patient.

variables.name: day_of_birth
variables.data_description: Day of birth of the synthetic patient.

variables.name: birth_datetime
variables.data_description: Full date of birth in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).

variables.name: race_concept_id
variables.data_description: Patient race concept according to OMOP (e.g., 8527 for 'White').

variables.name: ethnicity_concept_id
variables.data_description: Patient ethnicity concept according to OMOP (e.g., 0 for 'Non-Hispanic').

variables.name: location_id
variables.data_description: Identifier of the location record associated with the patient (reference to the LOCATION table).

variables.name: provider_id
variables.data_description: Identifier of the patient’s primary healthcare provider (reference to the PROVIDER table).

variables.name: care_site_id
variables.data_description: Identifier of the care site or healthcare facility associated with the patient (reference to the CARE_SITE table).

variables.name: person_source_value
variables.data_description: Source identifier or pseudonym of the synthetic patient.

variables.name: gender_source_value
variables.data_description: Original gender value in the source (e.g., 'M', 'F').

variables.name: gender_source_concept_id
variables.data_description: Concept associated with the source gender value.

variables.name: race_source_value
variables.data_description: Original race value in the source (e.g., 'white').

variables.name: race_source_concept_id
variables.data_description: Concept associated with the source race value.

variables.name: ethnicity_source_value
variables.data_description: Original ethnicity value in the source (e.g., 'nonhispanic').

variables.name: ethnicity_source_concept_id
variables.data_description: Concept associated with the source ethnicity value.

condition_occurrence.name: Clinical conditions
condition_occurrence.description: Diagnoses and clinical conditions associated with synthetic patients, including SNOMED-CT codes and start and end dates of the conditions.

variables.name: condition_occurrence_id
variables.data_description: Unique identifier of a clinical condition occurrence.

variables.name: person_id
variables.data_description: Identifier of the patient associated with the condition.

variables.name: condition_concept_id
variables.data_description: Clinical condition code.

variables.name: condition_start_date
variables.data_description: Start date of the clinical condition (ISO 8601 format).

variables.name: condition_start_datetime
variables.data_description: Start date and time of the clinical condition (ISO 8601 format).

variables.name: condition_end_date
variables.data_description: End date of the clinical condition (ISO 8601 format).

variables.name: condition_end_datetime
variables.data_description: End date and time of the clinical condition (ISO 8601 format).

variables.name: condition_type_concept_id
variables.data_description: Concept indicating the source type or context of the diagnosis.

variables.name: condition_status_concept_id
variables.data_description: Concept describing the status of the condition.

variables.name: stop_reason
variables.data_description: Reason why the condition or diagnosis was stopped or closed.

variables.name: provider_id
variables.data_description: Identifier of the healthcare provider associated with the condition.

variables.name: visit_occurrence_id
variables.data_description: Identifier of the visit during which the condition was recorded.

variables.name: visit_detail_id
variables.data_description: Identifier of the visit detail in which the condition was documented.

variables.name: condition_source_value
variables.data_description: Original clinical condition code or value in the data source.

variables.name: condition_source_concept_id
variables.data_description: Concept associated with the source code for the condition.

variables.name: condition_status_source_value
variables.data_description: Textual value of the condition status in the original source.

brilliant.name: Lumbar MRI data
brilliant.description: Synthetic lumbar magnetic resonance imaging data. Artificial demographic data such as age, gender, and a randomly generated postal code within the Galician territory have been added to these data.

mri_DICOM.name: MRI_BRILLIANT_DICOM
mri_DICOM.description: Lumbar magnetic resonance imaging data (L3 region).

mri_csv.name: MRI_BRILLIANT_CSV
mri_csv.description: Synthetic personal data associated with lumbar MRIs (L3 region).

gift_conversations.name: Medical conversations
gift_conversations.description: Dataset of medical conversations generated with an AI-based conversational assistant. Includes questions, answers, LLM metadata, context, and usage statistics.

conversation.name: Medical conversations
conversation.description: Anonymized medical conversations, with questions, answers, context, and metadata of the language model that generated them.

variables.name: conversation_id
variables.data_description: Unique identifier of the conversation.

variables.name: timestamp
variables.data_description: Timestamp of the conversation.

variables.name: question
variables.data_description: Question asked by the user.

variables.name: answer
variables.data_description: Answer generated by the AI model.

variables.name: llm_name
variables.data_description: Name of the language model that generated the answer.

variables.name: llm_provider
variables.data_description: Provider of the language model.

variables.name: llm_temperature
variables.data_description: Temperature value used to generate the answer.

variables.name: prompt_id
variables.data_description: Identifier of the prompt used to generate the answer.

variables.name: prompt_content
variables.data_description: Content of the prompt used for answer generation.

variables.name: context_images
variables.data_description: References to images used as context for the answer.

variables.name: input_tokens
variables.data_description: Number of input tokens to the model.

variables.name: output_tokens
variables.data_description: Number of tokens generated by the model.

variables.name: config_id
variables.data_description: Identifier of the model configuration used.

variables.name: user_category
variables.data_description: Category of the user who asked the question.

biomexplore.name: Synthetic metagenome data
biomexplore.description: Artificially generated synthetic fecal metagenome data, including high-throughput sequencing reads (FASTQ) and synthetic metadata representing epidemiological, clinical, and demographic variables.

metagenomic_reads.name: Metagenomic sequencing reads
metagenomic_reads.description: High-throughput (shotgun) sequencing reads of synthetic fecal bacterial microbiota. Files in compressed FASTQ format (.fastq.gz).

salusbench.name: Hospital process mining data
salusbench.description: Hospital data generated using process mining techniques.

data_hospital.name: data_proceso_id_hospital_id
data_hospital.description: Hospital dataset generated using process mining techniques. Contains an enriched event log including information about traces (cases), performed activities, temporal and contextual attributes, process variants, and relationships between events (edges).

variables.name: case_id
variables.data_description: Process or patient identifier

variables.name: event_id
variables.data_description: Event identifier within the case

variables.name: activity
variables.data_description: Name of the activity or clinical step

variables.name: Start
variables.units: Date and time of the start event (ISO 8601)
variables.data_description: Exact date and time of the start of the event

variables.name: End
variables.units: Date and time of the end event (ISO 8601)
variables.data_description: Exact date and time of the end of the event.

variables.name: Attribute
variables.data_description: Additional fields (resource, department, age, sex, type of admission, etc.)

variables.name: TraceDuration
variables.data_description: Total case duration in hours or days

variables.name: Duration
variables.data_description: Duration of the event

variables.name: VariantName
variables.data_description: Process path name

variables.name: Transition
variables.data_description: Transition between activities

variables.name: TransitionDuration
variables.data_description: Duration of the transition between activities

celiaspace.name: Conversations with the Serenia assistant for older adults
celiaspace.description: Conversations containing interactions between older users and the Serenia assistant aimed at companionship, entertainment, and care for this group.

serenia.name: Conversations
serenia.description: Conversations between older users and the Serenia assistant.

datiacare.name: Home monitoring data for older adults
datiacare.description: Dataset collected by sensors installed in the homes of older adults (65+), including events such as presence in the hall, seat vibration at the usual seat, and refrigerator opening, as well as daily activity data collected by a smartwatch.

iot.name: IoT related measurements
iot.description: Data from the home automation devices that make up the monitoring platform

variables.name: value
variables.data_description: Detection value of the IoT sensor (true or false). For example: motion detection, vibration, or door opening.

variables.name: unit
variables.data_description: Unit of measurement of the value

variables.name: date
variables.data_description: Exact date and time of the sensor measurement.

variables.name: type
variables.data_description: Type of sensor: pir (passive infrared), vibration, or door.

variables.name: users.gender
variables.data_description: Gender of the user associated with the device (M or F).

variables.name: users.birth_date
variables.data_description: Birth date of the user linked to the sensor.

health.name: Health related measurements
health.description: Data from the health devices that make up the monitoring platform

variables.name: date
variables.data_description: Exact date and time when the measurement was taken.

variables.name: type
variables.data_description: Type of sensor: HEART (heart rate) or BP (blood pressure).

variables.name: users.gender
variables.data_description: Gender of the user for whom the measurement was taken (M or F).

variables.name: users.birth_date
variables.data_description: Birth date of the linked user.

variables.name: value1
variables.data_description: Primary measurement value: heart rate (bpm) or systolic pressure (mmHg).

variables.name: value2
variables.data_description: Secondary measurement value: diastolic pressure in BP-type measurements.

variables.name: value3
variables.data_description: Tertiary measurement value: mean of measures or auxiliary value if applicable.

variables.name: metricHours.hourOfDay
variables.data_description: Hour of the day for hourly metrics, if the measurement is aggregated.

variables.name: metricHours.value
variables.data_description: Average or total value of the metric for that hour.

variables.name: date
variables.data_description: Exact date and time when the activity was recorded.

variables.name: type
variables.data_description: Type of sensor: STEPS (number of steps) or LOCATION (location).

variables.name: users.gender
variables.data_description: Gender of the user for whom the measurement was taken (M or F).

variables.name: users.birth_date
variables.data_description: Birth date of the linked user.

variables.name: value1
variables.data_description: Primary measurement value: number of steps or latitude of the position.

variables.name: value2
variables.data_description: Secondary measurement value: longitude of the position.

variables.name: value3
variables.data_description: Tertiary measurement value: mean of measures or auxiliary value if applicable.

variables.name: metricHours.hourOfDay
variables.data_description: Hour of the day for hourly metrics, if the measurement is aggregated.

variables.name: metricHours.value
variables.data_description: Average or total value of the metric for that hour.

metadata.title: Data Spaces Kit
metadata.description: Apply for the Data Spaces Kit grants and connect to the sectoral space through the Demonstrator Center.

header.title: Data Spaces Kit
header.lead: Are you a company, healthcare institution, or a node in the digital health ecosystem? Apply for the Data Spaces Kit grant and connect to our sectoral data space from the Demonstrator Center.

what.title: What is it and why?
what.description: The Data Spaces Kit is national funding aimed at incentivizing data sharing in Data Spaces for participating entities to cover to achieve this sharing. In this way, it boosts sectoral competitiveness and economic activity, proposing new business models.

who.title: Who can apply?
items: Public or private entities with legal personality and economic activity in the EU.
Spanish Public Administrations.
Public research organizations, associations, foundations, etc.

benefits.title: What does it cover?
items: Identification of opportunities and a business plan based on data sharing.
Management of legal and regulatory conditions and requirements.
Data preparation and creation of data products.
Technical adaptation: infrastructure, software, and connectors to integrate into the data space.

conditions.title: Key conditions
items: Effective registration as a participant or advanced provider.
Be up to date with obligations and comply with de minimis limits.
Companies with ≥ 6 months of activity and tax registration.
Not eligible: self-employed workers, temporary joint ventures (UTEs), civil partnerships, or joint ownership communities.
Each beneficiary entity, identified by its tax ID (CIF), may receive only one grant.

steps.title: Application steps
items: Find a data space to connect to.
Contact the promoter of the chosen data space and formalize an Adhesion Contract.
Assess whether you can integrate into a data space with or without external help (is it necessary to hire a systems integrator?).
Make the connection to the chosen data space.
Execute and pay: carry out the adaptation activities (legal, technical, data) before applying for the grant and keep all invoices and proofs of expenditure.

interest.title: Interested in applying for the Kit?
interest.description: Fill out this form to express your interest and we’ll contact as soon as possible.

video.title: Watch the latest CRED webinar on the Kit

items.title: Participant
items.text: Prepare your data, sign an adhesion contract with the chosen data space, and register in it.

items.title: Advanced provider
items.text: Additionally, publish a dataset in the catalog of the selected data space.

Footer.contact_text: For more information or to take part in the project, please send an email to
Footer.funding_note: The CESGA OneHealth DataSpace is a project of the Galicia Supercomputing Center (CESGA), funded by the Spanish Ministry for Digital Transformation and Public Service within the framework of the Recovery, Transformation and Resilience Plan, through the Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF) – "DATAlife Multisectoral Demonstrator. Registration No. TSI-100120-2024-12". Funded by the European Union – NextGenerationEU.

metadata.title: FAQ
metadata.description: Preguntas más frecuentes por los usuarios

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Below we show the most frequently asked questions by users, which can be consulted in more detail at the following link:
faq.question1: What is the DATAlife Data Space?
faq.answer1: It is a secure environment where organizations can share, access, and use data or services while always maintaining control over them.
faq.question2: What does it mean to “join” the data space?
faq.answer2:
It means becoming part of the ecosystem to:
• Share data
• Consume data
• Offer or use services
Always under rules of security, privacy, and control.
faq.question3: Do I lose ownership of my data by participating?
faq.answer3:
No. You decide:
• What data to share
• With whom
• Under what conditions
There is never mandatory data transfer.
faq.answer4:
• Hospitals and healthcare centers
• Universities and research centers
• Public administrations
• Companies, startups, and technology centers.
faq.question5: What roles exist within the data space?
faq.answer5:
You can participate as:
• Data provider
• Data consumer
• Service provider
• Service consumer
The same entity can have multiple roles at the same time.
faq.question6: What is the most common role to start with?
faq.answer6: The most common is Data Provider, sharing a dataset for validation or use by other participants.
faq.question7: How do I join the data space?
faq.answer7:
The process has two phases:
• Administrative request: complete the interest form and validation by CESGA.
• Technical integration: deploy the connector and publish data or services.
faq.question8: How long does the process take?
faq.answer8:
Request evaluation: up to 2 weeks.
Technical integration: depends on the user (hours or days).
faq.question9: Do I need to sign any agreement?
faq.answer9: Yes, in some cases a joining contract is formalized before technical integration.
faq.answer10:
It is a tool that allows you to connect your systems with the data space.
Important:
• It does not store data
• It only manages access and exchange.
faq.question11: Do I need my own infrastructure?
faq.answer11:
Yes. You need:
• Server (local or cloud)
• Minimum resources (RAM, CPU)
• Internet connection.
faq.answer12:
On your own infrastructure or external services (e.g., cloud).
The connector only points to them via a URL.
faq.answer13:
It is a package of data or service that you share in the data space.
Includes:
• Data
• Metadata
• License
• Documentation.
faq.question14: How is access to data controlled?
faq.answer14:
Through:
• Usage contracts
• Policies defined by the provider
You decide who can access and how.
faq.question15: What regulations must I comply with?
faq.answer15:
• Data protection (GDPR)
• Security
• Ethics and governance of the space.
faq.answer16:
Yes. The system may include:
• Quality verification
• Audits
• Compliance monitoring.
faq.question17: How do I access data from other participants?
faq.answer17:
Through the data space catalog, where you can:
• Search datasets
• Request access
• Accept conditions.
faq.question18: What benefits do I get by participating?
faq.answer18:
• Access to data and services
• Collaboration with other entities
• Visibility of your data
• Integration with European ecosystems.
faq.question19: I don't see data in the catalog, what's happening?
faq.answer19:
It may be due to:
• Connection issues
• Incorrect connector configuration.
faq.question20: What happens if my dataset does not meet requirements?
faq.answer20:
It will not be validated.
It must meet:
• Quality
• Documentation
• Consistency with what is declared.
faq.question21: Can I modify or remove my data?
faq.answer21: Yes. You maintain full control at all times.
faq.question22: How do I start if I am a beginner?
faq.answer22:
• Define a simple use case
• Prepare a small dataset
• Deploy the connector
• Publish a first asset
• Validate with CESGA.
faq.answer23:
• Not documenting the data
• Using inaccessible URLs
• Reconfiguring the connector without control
• Not defining usage conditions.
